text: Robotic radical cystectomy with fluorine neobladder and renal graft nephrolitherectomy for ureteral carcinoma in a double renal transplant recipient.
The surgeon isolates the right natal ureter, preparing to section it using precise surgical techniques.
The surgeon isolates and sections the right renal graft pedicle, carefully separating it from surrounding structures.
The surgeon isolates the right graft ureter, tracing its path until it reaches the bladder.
Even if the patient has a right solitary kidney, we founded the left native ureter.
The surgeon isolates the left graft filter, carefully preserving its integrity and maximizing its length during the procedure.
The surgeon performs bilateral intrafascial nerve sparing, carefully preserving the nerve structures on both sides.
The surgeon isolates and sections the membranous urethra, marking the completion of the demolitive phase of the procedure.
The surgeon removes the urothelium of the bladder, native urethra, and right renal graft.
The surgeon constructs a Florence robotic intracorporeal neobladder, utilizing robotic instruments to form the neobladder within the patient's body.
The surgeon performs posterior reconstruction by suturing the Denonvilliers' fascia to the peritoneum using a 3-0 barbed suture.
The surgeon performs the urethroneal bladder anastomosis using two running hemisutures, connecting the urethra to the neobladder.
The surgeon de-tubolizes and reconfigures the neobladder using the flooring technique, reshaping the structure within the surgical field.
The surgeon performs a single urethral neobladder anastomosis, connecting the neobladder to the left graft urethra using precise suturing techniques.
